description:
You are a security engineer in charge of rotating credentials that are injected into containers at run-time.

Current setup (already present when you start):
1. File /home/user/container_secrets.env contains exactly two lines  
   DB_USER=legacy  
   DB_PASS=OldP@ssword123
2. No file called /home/user/rotation.log exists yet.

New credentials to be added:
DB_USER=appuser  
DB_PASS=StrongerP@55w0rd!2024

Your objectives (accomplish them with a single, self-contained shell command):
A. Append the two new credential lines, in the order shown above, to the end of /home/user/container_secrets.env. The original two legacy lines must stay untouched and remain as the first two lines of the file.
B. Create the file /home/user/rotation.log containing exactly one line, in the precise format below (including the terminating newline character):

2024-01-01T00:00:00Z ROTATED

Automated verification will:
• Confirm that /home/user/container_secrets.env now contains four lines, in the exact order listed (legacy credentials first, new credentials last).
• Confirm that /home/user/rotation.log exists and contains exactly the single line shown above followed by a newline.
Use any tools available in the terminal, but fulfil both objectives with just one compound command executed at the prompt.
truth:
Initial state before the agent runs:
• /home/user/container_secrets.env  
  Content (with trailing newline):  
  DB_USER=legacy\n  
  DB_PASS=OldP@ssword123\n

Expected final state after the agent finishes:
• /home/user/container_secrets.env  
  Content (with trailing newline):  
  DB_USER=legacy\n  
  DB_PASS=OldP@ssword123\n  
  DB_USER=appuser\n  
  DB_PASS=StrongerP@55w0rd!2024\n

• /home/user/rotation.log  
  Content (with trailing newline):  
  2024-01-01T00:00:00Z ROTATED\n
name: task_000000_3b508773
